tree_title: Mastering the 7 Stages of Film Production

overall_topic: Mastering the 7 Stages of Film Production
questions: In the context of mastering the seven stages of film production—where the development stage centers on creating the screenplay and the producing role spans financing, marketing, and distribution—identify the specific educational division of a major U.S.-based film academy dedicated to training screenwriters, as well as the academy’s fully online master’s degree designed for entrepreneurial producers. For both, provide a detailed comparison that includes: (1) the types of degrees or programs offered for screenwriters, including locations and online options; (2) examples of workshop formats or specializations available to writing students; and (3) for the online producing master’s degree, its curriculum structure, types of hands-on projects completed, program duration, and tuition model.

logic_chain: Mastering the 7 Stages of Film Production
rounds.question: In the context of mastering the seven stages of film production—where the development stage centers on creating the screenplay and the producing role spans financing, marketing, and distribution—identify the specific educational division of a major U.S.-based film academy dedicated to training screenwriters, as well as the academy’s fully online master’s degree designed for entrepreneurial producers. For both, provide a detailed comparison that includes: (1) the types of degrees or programs offered for screenwriters, including locations and online options; (2) examples of workshop formats or specializations available to writing students; and (3) for the online producing master’s degree, its curriculum structure, types of hands-on projects completed, program duration, and tuition model.
checklist_width: List of screenwriting degrees (AFA, BFA, MFA, BFA in Entertainment Media concentration, Online MA in Screenwriting for Alternative Media)
Mention of 1-Year Screenwriting Program and its availability in New York and Los Angeles
Examples of in-person and online screenwriting workshops (e.g., feature writing, comedy, TV writing, comic book writing, 15-week online workshops, 4-week online workshops)
Identification of the Online MA in Entrepreneurial Producing and Innovation as a three-semester program
Description of curriculum areas (Producing Essentials, Line Producing and Production Accounting, The Art of Pitching, finance, marketing, distribution, entertainment law, history/theory)
Examples of required producing projects (short film, finance plans, marketing/distribution plans, series bible, pitch deck, business plan, capstone e-portfolio)
Tuition structure ($10,000 per semester plus technology fee, total duration three semesters)
checklist_depth: NYFA Screenwriting School + Correctly identified as the division focused on training screenwriters in support of the development stage of film production
Online MA in Entrepreneurial Producing and Innovation + Correctly identified as the producing-focused graduate program aligned with financing, marketing, and distribution stages
rounds.rationale: The question uses the logic of the seven stages of film production to indirectly point to two distinct but related academic units: one centered on screenplay development (deep reasoning tied to the development stage) and one centered on producing across financing, marketing, and distribution. It requires broad aggregation of details across both entities—degrees, workshops, curriculum, projects, duration, and tuition—ensuring the respondent must synthesize information from multiple sources rather than a single reference.

rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: NYFA Screenwriting School Screenwriting School Hands-On Degrees, Programs, and Workshops In film, television, and new media, the Screenwriter establishes the basic building bl
rounds.answer_a:
The New York Film Academy (NYFA) houses the NYFA Screenwriting School, dedicated to training writers, and offers the fully online Master of Arts (MA) in Entrepreneurial Producing and Innovation for aspiring producers [1]. 

For screenwriters, the NYFA Screenwriting School offers Bachelor of Fine Arts (BFA), Master of Fine Arts (MFA), and Associate of Fine Arts (AFA) degrees, alongside 1-Year and 2-Year Certificate programs [1]. These programs are available at physical campuses in New York City, Los Angeles, Miami (South Beach), and the Gold Coast of Australia, with fully online alternatives also available [2]. Writing students can engage in immersive workshops spanning 1, 4, 8, or 12 weeks, specializing in highly targeted disciplines such as feature films, television (including spec scripts and original pilots), comic book writing, and video game writing [1][2]. 

In contrast, the MA in Entrepreneurial Producing and Innovation is specifically designed as an accelerated 1-year (three-semester), 100% online program focusing strictly on the executive mechanics of film production: finance, marketing, and distribution [3]. Its curriculum structure translates executive development into a hybrid format, blending asynchronous foundational modules with live, synchronous interactive sessions [3]. Instead of physical on-set production, students complete extensive entrepreneurial hands-on projects. Notably, producing students synthesize their studies by developing comprehensive business plans for modern media companies, creating detailed financing pitch decks for original slates of intellectual property, and drafting digital transmedia bibles to secure funding [3]. Financially, the program's tuition model operates on a per-semester flat-fee basis for full-time remote enrollment, diverging from variable per-credit costs to offer a predictable financial framework for working professionals [4].

## References
[1] New York Film Academy. "Screenwriting School & Programs." Retrieved from https://www.nyfa.edu/screenwriting-school/
[2] New York Film Academy. "Screenwriting Workshops." Retrieved from https://www.nyfa.edu/screenwriting-school/workshops/
[3] New York Film Academy. "Online Master of Arts (MA) in Entrepreneurial Producing and Innovation." Retrieved from https://www.nyfa.edu/online-programs/ma-entrepreneurial-producing-innovation/
[4] New York Film Academy. "NYFA Online Tuition." Retrieved from https://www.nyfa.edu/admissions/tuition/online/

rounds.judge_reasoning:
First, Deep Logic: Agent A correctly identified the NYFA Screenwriting School as the division dedicated to screenwriters and the Online MA in Entrepreneurial Producing and Innovation as the producing-focused graduate program. Agent B provided only a list of unrelated references (USC, NYU, AFI, UCLA) and did not answer the query at all—failing the core entity requirement. 

Second, Width/Completeness: Agent A covered most required elements—degree types (AFA, BFA, MFA), locations and online options, workshop formats and specializations, three-semester structure, curriculum focus areas (finance, marketing, distribution), hands-on producing projects, and tuition model structure (per-semester flat fee). While some checklist specifics (e.g., exact $10,000 tuition figure, named courses like Line Producing and Production Accounting, specific project artifacts like capstone e-portfolio) were not fully enumerated, there were no clear hallucinations. Agent B failed to address any checklist items.

Finally, User Experience: Agent A provided a structured, informative response with contextual explanation and citations. Agent B offered no substantive content. Given that Agent A met the core logic and delivered a usable, detailed answer while Agent B failed entirely, the outcome is A_MUCH_BETTER.
